alg.purpose:
The partitioning problem is central to Quicksort. The choice of partitioning algorithm affects
whether the algorithm is in-place and whether it is stable, and can also influence the worst-case
time complexity.
Naive 3-way partition algorithm
The naive 3-way partitioning algorithm simply partitions the array into three temporary arrays,
one for elements that are less than the pivot, one for elements that are greater than the pivot,
and one for elements equal to the pivot. A pseudocode for naive 3-way partitioning is shown as
Algorithm 15.
Algorithm 15Naive 3-way partitioning
1:functionPARTITION(array[lo..hi], pivot)
